mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.0%, labor market tightness triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and wage inflation, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during low-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 60d returns during late-cycle phases, or rolling Rank IC drops below 0.03 when the macro regime shifts to monetary tightening.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, which directly supports earnings growth forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth coincides with negative 60d returns in >60% of observations, or the signal predictive accuracy vanishes with Rank IC below 0.02 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.3% signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation months consistently precede positive 20d returns, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge with Rank IC below 0.01 over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with rising federal funds rates creates a stagflationary risk premium, as strong labor demand fuels wage pressures while monetary tightening restricts liquidity, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive returns in >65% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in housing starts act as a leading indicator for consumer spending and financial sector health, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week during periods of accommodative credit conditions.
failure_condition: Housing start spikes fail to predict positive 5d returns when credit spreads widen, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.02 during tightening regimes.
